name: Quarter-Wave Monopole (wire)
description: A quarter-wavelength vertical wire antenna mounted over a ground plane, representing half of a dipole antenna. It exhibits omnidirectional radiation pattern in the horizontal plane with vertical polarization. The ground plane acts as an image plane, creating the effect of a full half-wave dipole.

length.name: Physical Length
length.description: Physical length of the monopole wire

wire_radius.name: Wire Radius
wire_radius.description: Radius of the monopole wire conductor

ground_plane_radius.name: Ground Plane Radius
ground_plane_radius.description: Radius of the circular ground plane

feed_height.name: Feed Height
feed_height.description: Height of feed point above ground plane

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves determining the physical length for resonance, selecting appropriate wire diameter, sizing the ground plane, and optimizing the feed structure for desired impedance matching.

steps.title: Calculate Resonant Length
steps.description: Determine the physical length for quarter-wave resonance including end effects

steps.title: Select Wire Diameter
steps.description: Choose wire radius based on bandwidth and mechanical requirements

steps.title: Size Ground Plane
steps.description: Design ground plane radius for proper radiation pattern and impedance

steps.title: Calculate Input Impedance
steps.description: Determine theoretical input impedance at resonance

steps.title: Design Feed Structure
steps.description: Design coaxial feed connection and matching network if needed

steps.title: Optimize for Bandwidth
steps.description: Adjust wire diameter and length for desired bandwidth

equations.resonantFrequency: f0 = c / (4 * L_eff) where L_eff = L + delta_L
equations.inputImpedance: Zin = 36.5 + j * 21.25 * tan(beta * L) for thin wire over infinite ground
equations.gain: G = 5.15 dBi for infinite ground plane, G = 2.15 dBi for small ground plane
equations.radiationPattern: E_theta = j * 60 * I0 * cos(beta * L * cos(theta)) / (r * sin(theta))

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Method of moments with thin wire approximation and image theory for ground plane effects
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Far-field integration using current distribution I(z) = I0 * cos(beta * z) with image currents

references: Balanis, C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 4
Stutzman, W.L. & Thiele, G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 4
Kraus, J.D. & Marhefka, R.J. 'Antennas for All Applications', 3rd Edition, Chapter 8
Volakis, J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 5
